frameworks.name: Bayesian function learning
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian function-learning framework adapted to discrete graph-structured input spaces.

frameworks.name: graph diffusion
frameworks.one_line: Generalization modeled as diffusion of observed function values across graph nodes.

frameworks.name: heuristic models
frameworks.one_line: Simpler heuristic accounts of generalization on graphs used as baselines.

core_question: How do humans learn and generalize functions defined on graph-structured spaces?
key_assumptions: Relationships between items are defined by graph connectivity
Generalization can be cast as a Bayesian inference problem over functions
Function values propagate along graph edges via a diffusion process
manipulations: Graph structure of a virtual subway network used for predictions
Observed function (passenger volume) values at sampled nodes
key_findings: Diffusion-based Bayesian model captures human generalization on the graph
The model also accounts for participants' confidence judgments
Diffusion model quantitatively outperforms several heuristic alternatives
interpretations: People exploit graph connectivity structure when generalizing functions in complex discrete spaces
